NCPOR Marks World Environment Day 2026 with Science, Sustainability and Community Action
articles.full_content:
Over 400 Participants Join Scientific Outreach, Climate Awareness Programmes and ‘Ek Ped Maa Ke Naam’ Plantation Drive
Vasco-Goa, June 7, 2026 ﻿
The National Centre for Polar and Ocean Research (NCPOR), an autonomous institute under the Ministry of Earth Sciences, celebrated World Environment Day 2026 with a vibrant series of scientific outreach programmes, sustainability initiatives and community engagement activities at its campus in Vasco, Goa.﻿
The celebrations highlighted the importance of environmental stewardship, climate action and sustainable living while showcasing NCPOR’s pioneering work in polar and ocean sciences.﻿
The programme was inaugurated by Prof. M. K. Janarthanam, Chairman, Goa State Research Foundation (GSRF), who delivered the keynote address on “Environment and Sustainability.” He emphasized the need to protect biodiversity and advance the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) through collective action and environmental responsibility.﻿
Addressing the gathering, Dr. Thamban Meloth, Director, NCPOR, underscored the critical role of polar research in understanding climate change and strengthening India’s environmental resilience. He highlighted the importance of science, innovation and public participation in addressing global environmental challenges.﻿
A special lecture by Prof. Naresh Chandra Pant (Retd.), University of Delhi and Consultant, NCPOR, focused on the need for a revised geoscientific perspective for sustainable development, emphasizing evidence-based policymaking and responsible resource management.﻿
One of the major attractions of the event was the immersive Science on a Sphere session, which enabled participants to explore Earth’s oceans, atmosphere, climate systems and polar regions through dynamic visualizations, enhancing public understanding of global environmental processes.﻿
As part of the celebrations, NCPOR organised the nationwide campaign “Ek Ped Maa Ke Naam” , inspired by the Hon’ble Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi’s Mission LiFE initiative. The plantation drive began with Dr. Thamban Meloth planting the first sapling and concluded with the planting of over 100 saplings by scientists, students, staff members and guests.﻿
The event brought together nearly 100 participants from 35 organisations across Goa, alongside more than 300 NCPOR scientists, staff members, students and members of the public. ﻿
Source - NCPOR PRO/PIB Panaji/AY/PK

MoS Environment, Forest and Climate Change Launches "NZP Saathi App" and Inaugurates Self-ticketing Kiosks at National Zoological Park
articles.full_content:
New digital initiatives aimed at enhancing visitor convenience through smart navigation and seamless ticket booking
Union Minister of State for Environment, Forest and Climate Change Shri Kirti Vardhan Singh today launched the "NZP Saathi App", a smart digital zoo guide, and inaugurated Self-ticketing Kiosks at the National Zoological Park (NZP), New Delhi, aimed at enhancing visitor experience through smart navigation and easing online ticket booking.
The Minister visited an exhibition showcasing creative works prepared by students who participated in the recently concluded Summer Vacation Programme 2026. He appreciated the creativity of the young participants and commended the zoo authorities for organising educational and awareness programmes that foster a deeper understanding of wildlife conservation among students.
The Minister formally launched the "NZP Saathi App" and inaugurated the Self-ticketing Kiosks, marking a significant step towards visitor convenience and digital transformation at the National Zoological Park.
The "NZP Saathi App", available on both Android and iOS platforms, features an interactive digital map and smart navigation system that enables visitors to locate animal enclosures, washrooms, buggy points, exits and other facilities. The app also offers thematic tours, including Express Tour, Family Tour, Grand Zoo Tour and personalised "My Tour" options.
The Self-ticketing Kiosks will facilitate online ticket booking through UPI-based digital payments with free Wi-Fi connectivity for on-the-spot ticket booking.
Following the inauguration, the Minister held a meeting with officials and section in-charges to review ongoing initiatives and discuss strategies for the overall enhancement, modernisation and efficient management of the National Zoological Park.
The Minister also inspected the bird houses and reviewed the summer management arrangements for the avian collection. He assessed the enclosure conditions and offered suggestions for further improving animal welfare and habitat management.
During his visit to the Veterinary Hospital, the Minister observed the care being provided to in-house sick animals and held detailed discussions with zoo veterinarians and animal health staff regarding healthcare and management practices.
Later, the Minister visited the lion enclosure and observed the Asiatic lions, Kartik and Karani. He expressed satisfaction with their upkeep and the overall management of the enclosure.
The Minister lauded the staff and officials of the National Zoological Park for their commitment, dedication and continuous efforts towards wildlife conservation, animal welfare and public education.
The National Zoological Park remains committed to strengthening conservation education, improving visitor services through technology and ensuring the highest standards of animal care and management.

Lok Sabha Speaker Shri Om Birla today asserted that the vision of Viksit Bharat 2047 can only be realized through public participation, innovation, entrepreneurship, and collective resolve. He noted that India’s journey toward becoming a developed nation must be guided by inclusive growth, social responsibility, and active citizen engagement.

He observed that the community has consistently worked for societal welfare by establishing and nurturing institutions dedicated to education, healthcare, and public service across generations and regions.

Whether in metropolitan centers, the North-East, or southern India, community members have created opportunities, generated employment, and strengthened local economies. He added that the community’s commitment to honesty, ethical conduct, and social harmony has set exemplary standards in the business world.

Chief Minister Nayab Singh Saini to Inaugurate ‘Khadi Rozgar Utsav’ in Siwani on June 17
articles.full_content:
KVIC Chairman Manoj Goyal Meets Chief Minister in Chandigarh, Extends the First Invitation
In line with Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s vision of Vocal for Local, Atmanirbhar Bharat, and Viksit Bharat, the Khadi and Village Industries Commission (KVIC), under the Ministry of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises, Government of India, will organize the grand ‘Khadi Rozgar Utsav’ on June 17, 2026, at the Government Model Sanskriti Senior Secondary School premises in Siwani, Bhiwani district, Haryana. The event will be inaugurated by Haryana Chief Minister Shri Nayab Singh Saini. During the programme, machines and toolkits will be distributed to beneficiaries under various schemes, while selected beneficiaries of the Prime Minister’s Employment Generation Programme (PMEGP) will be handed over margin money sanction letters.
As part of the preparations, on Saturday, KVIC Chairman Shri Manoj Goyal paid a courtesy visit to Chief Minister Shri Nayab Singh Saini in Chandigarh and presented him with the first invitation for the event, which has been accepted by the Chief Minister gladly.
On the occasion, Shri Manoj Goyal said that under the leadership of Chief Minister Shri Nayab Singh Saini, Haryana has been witnessing remarkable progress in rural development, employment generation, women’s empowerment, promotion of cottage industries, and encouragement of local products. These sustained efforts are continuously creating new opportunities for the economic and social upliftment of rural communities.
He further stated that the ‘Khadi Karigar Mahotsav’, held in Rohtak on October 3, 2025, had emerged as a celebration of the dignity, self-respect, and commitment of lakhs of workers associated with khadi. The event received the valuable guidance of Union Home and Cooperation Minister Shri Amit Shah, while Chief Minister Shri Nayab Singh Saini led the entire programme with dedication, warmth, and a spirit of service that proved highly inspiring. It was at that moment that the resolve was made to organize the next major event dedicated to the honour, employment, and empowerment of khadi artisans once again on the sacred land of Haryana.
Shri Goyal said that the ‘Khadi Rozgar Utsav’ in Siwani would herald a new era of rural economy, indigenous entrepreneurship, and self-reliance through khadi in Haryana. The dignified presence of the Chief Minister will serve as a source of enthusiasm, energy, and inspiration for artisans, youth, and rural families coming from the remote areas of southern Haryana.
He informed that under the leadership of Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi and the visionary guidance of Union Home and Cooperation Minister Shri Amit Shah, the khadi and village industries sector achieved a record turnover of ₹1.87 lakh crore during the financial year 2025–26, creating a new milestone. The sector has now set an ambitious target of achieving a turnover of ₹2.51 lakh crore.
